confidence_notes: High confidence for full names in standard prose. May miss initials-only, nicknames, or names without surrounding context. Recall-biased — flags PERSON entities broadly.
Covers cities, counties, zip codes, street addresses via spaCy NER. Does not enforce 'smaller than state' at detection time — post-processing filtering recommended for state-level false positives.

Covers birth dates, admission/discharge dates, date of death, and other date formats (MM/DD/YYYY, written month, ISO 8601). Includes year by default — HIPAA requires year-only dates to be de-identified separately if needed.
Covers US and international phone formats including dashes, parentheses, dots, and extensions. Shared recognizer with fax numbers (identifier #5).
Fax numbers follow the same format as phone numbers. No separate recognizer needed. Context keywords ('fax', 'facsimile') may help distinguish in post-processing if differentiation is required.
High confidence. Standard RFC 5322 email pattern. Smoke-tested and confirmed working (Proof Unit 2, Task #2).
Covers SSNs in NNN-NN-NNNN and NNNNNNNNN formats. High precision.

No native Presidio recognizer for patient MRNs. Custom implementation uses alphanumeric patterns (e.g., MRN-XXXXXX, 7-10 digit numeric) anchored by context keywords: 'MRN', 'medical record', 'patient ID', 'chart number'. Recall-biased.
No native Presidio recognizer. Custom implementation uses alphanumeric ID patterns anchored by context keywords: 'beneficiary', 'member ID', 'plan ID', 'insurance ID', 'subscriber'. Recall-biased.

Presidio's US_BANK_NUMBER covers US bank routing/account formats. Custom ACCOUNT_NUMBER adds broader recall for non-bank accounts (utility, retail, medical billing) using numeric patterns anchored by context keywords: 'account', 'acct', 'account number', 'account #'. Both recognizers active simultaneously.

Presidio natively covers MEDICAL_LICENSE (US medical professional license) and US_DRIVER_LICENSE (US driver's license). Custom CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER adds broader recall for professional certificates, occupational licenses, and non-US licenses using alphanumeric patterns with context keywords: 'license', 'certificate', 'cert', 'credential', 'lic #'. All three recognizers active simultaneously.

No native Presidio recognizer. Custom implementation covers: (1) VINs — strict 17-character NHTSA format with I/O/Q exclusion and position-based validation; (2) license plate patterns — US state plate formats. Context keywords: 'VIN', 'vehicle identification', 'plate', 'license plate'. High precision for VINs due to strict format.

No native Presidio recognizer. Custom implementation uses alphanumeric serial number patterns anchored by context keywords: 'serial', 'serial number', 'S/N', 'SN:', 'device ID', 'device serial', 'serial #'. Recall-biased due to high variability in serial number formats across manufacturers.
Covers http/https URLs and bare domain references. High confidence.
Covers IPv4 (dotted decimal) and IPv6 formats. High confidence.

No native Presidio recognizer. Best-effort text-description approach: detects mentions of biometric identifiers as noun phrases using keyword patterns: 'fingerprint', 'voiceprint', 'retina scan', 'iris scan', 'facial recognition', 'biometric', 'palm print', 'gait analysis'. High false-negative rate expected for non-descriptive references. Documented limitation — do not apply accuracy thresholds to this identifier.
Not applicable to text-based scanning. ScanMeNow is a text scanner. Image-based PHI detection is explicitly out of scope for Phase 1.

hipaa_identifier: Any other unique identifying number, characteristic, or code
confidence_notes: HIPAA catch-all clause. Not directly detectable as a discrete entity type. Coverage is provided indirectly by the combination of all 17 recognizers above — any structured identifier not caught by other recognizers may still be flagged if it matches a broader pattern (e.g., ACCOUNT_NUMBER or CERTIFICATE_LICENSE_NUMBER with recall-biased tuning).
